intro: Fernandezia capitata, also known as Kana or Kana root, is a plant native to the tropical regions of South America, belonging to the Gentianaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Bioactive constituents of Fernandezia capitata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
